doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_nan_p: NaN predicate. Returns true iff x is the NaN value, false for any other kind (normal, zero, inf). Trivial one-line port: reads the locked-schema kind discriminant directly. Class 'arithmetic' (50ms per-case budget).
divergence_from_c: C returns int (0/non-zero); TS returns boolean. Predicate never throws — `is NaN?` is well-defined for every well-formed MPFR kind including NaN itself. The C side dispatches on a sentinel exponent value (__MPFR_EXP_NAN) inspected by MPFR_IS_NAN; the TS locked schema makes the kind an explicit discriminant, so the port is a single field comparison.
refs: mpfr/src/isnan.c L24–L28 — the C reference (one-line body returning MPFR_IS_NAN(x)).
mpfr/tests/tisnan.c — source for the `mined` cases (exercises every kind plus signed zero / signed inf coverage).

CLAUDE.md "Hallucination-risk callouts: NaN ≠ NaN" — for nan_p the predicate is unambiguous (it returns true on NaN itself); the rule about IEEE-equality NaN traps applies to other functions, not to this one.
